    SampleSales — Audit & Health Report

    BI architects, technical leads, and governance teams · generated TIMESTAMP

    Documentation Completeness: 6% 16 fields awaiting input

    Missing: Owner, Refresh Schedule, Version, Status, Author, +11 more

    85/100
    Health Score
    Good
    Band
    Low
    Complexity
    14
    Findings

    This model scores 85/100 overall (Good), with low structural complexity across 4 tables, 4 measures, and 3 relationships. Modeling is the area holding the score back (70/100), while performance is the strongest area (97/100). 4 DAX finding(s), 6 failed best-practice check(s), 3 performance risk signal(s), and 7 governance finding(s) were identified. The top priority is: Some relationships use bidirectional cross-filtering. (High) — Switch to single-direction filtering and use CROSSFILTER() in specific DAX measures where bidirectional behavior is truly needed. ```csharp // Tabular Editor C# Script to resolve bidirectional cross-filtering Model.Relationships.First(rel => rel.FromTable.Name == "Sales" && rel.ToTable.Name == "Date").CrossFilteringBehavior = CrossFilteringBehavior.OneDirection; ```

    1. Overall Health Score

    85/100
    Good

    3. DAX Review

    4. Model Best Practices

    RuleCheckResultDetail
    PBIC-MOD-007Star schemaPassModel follows a star schema — a star schema centred on the 'Sales' fact table.
    PBIC-MOD-008Fact/dimension separationPass1 fact table(s), 2 dimension table(s).
    PBIC-NAM-001Table naming conventionsPassNo generic/default table names detected.
    PBIC-DOC-001Display folder usagePass4/4 measures use a display folder.
    PBIC-DOC-002Description coverageFail0/17 measures and visible columns have a description.
    PBIC-MOD-005Dedicated date tablePass'Date' looks like the model's date table.
    PBIC-MOD-006Hidden technical/key columnsFail3/6 ID/key-like columns are hidden.
    PBIC-MOD-010Unused calculated columnsFail1 calculated column(s) are not referenced by any visual: Sales[LineTotal].
    PBIC-MOD-009Inactive relationshipsFail1 inactive relationship(s) — only reachable via USERELATIONSHIP().
    PBIC-MOD-001Bidirectional cross-filteringFail1 relationship(s) use bidirectional cross-filtering, which can create ambiguous filter paths.
    PBIC-MOD-002Many-to-many relationshipsPassNo many-to-many relationships.
    PBIC-MOD-014Many-to-many relationship without a bridge tablePassEvery many-to-many relationship routes through a bridge table, or none exist.
    PBIC-MOD-015Bidirectional relationship filtering a fact tableFail1 bidirectional relationship(s) filter a fact table, a major performance and correctness risk: Sales <-> Date.
    PBIC-MOD-003No circular relationship pathsPassNo circular relationship paths detected.
    PBIC-MOD-004No disconnected fact/dimension tablesPassEvery fact/dimension table participates in at least one relationship.
    PBIC-NAM-002No development leftovers in production modelPassNo table/column names match common development-leftover patterns.
    PBIC-MOD-012Summarize-by setting on key columnsPassKey/ID columns have SummarizeBy set to None.
    PBIC-MOD-013Snowflake dimension depth limitPassAll dimension tables are within recommended chain depth of 2.
    PBIC-NAM-004Column naming conventions (typos)PassNo typos detected in column names.

    5. Performance Risks

    All performance risks below are heuristics inferred from metadata only — no row-level data is ever extracted, so nothing here reflects a measured runtime.

    RuleKindObjectTableSeverityDetail
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalCustomerKeySalesLow'CustomerKey' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalOrderDateKeySalesLow'OrderDateKey' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.
    PBIC-PERF-002High Cardinality SignalShipDateKeySalesLow'ShipDateKey' is named like an identifier and is visible — heuristic signal of high cardinality from naming/type only, not measured against actual data.

    6. Governance

    RuleAreaSeverityDetail
    PBIC-GOV-001RLSLowRole 'Regional Manager' has no members assigned in the model file (membership may be managed in the Power BI Service).
    PBIC-GOV-002DescriptionsMediumOnly 0% of measures/visible columns have a description.
    PBIC-GOV-003OwnershipMediumNo owner or data classification was specified when this documentation was generated.
    PBIC-GOV-006Unused PagesLowReport page 'Data Quality' is hidden and has no drill-through targets configured (potentially unused leftover).
    PBIC-GOV-009Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Sales' and 'Customer' references a hidden column.
    PBIC-GOV-009Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Sales' and 'Date' references a hidden column.
    PBIC-GOV-009Broken Rel Hidden ColLowRelationship between 'Sales' and 'Date' references a hidden column.

    7. Unused Assets

    Asset TypeCountItems
    Measures2Revenue YTD, Orphan Margin
    Columns8Customer[Customer Name], Date[Month Name], Sales[CustomerKey], Sales[OrderDateKey], Sales[ShipDateKey], Sales[Quantity], Sales[UnitPrice], Sales[Status]
    Tables0—
    Calculated columns1Sales[LineTotal]
    Report pages1Data Quality

    Hierarchies and calculation groups are not yet parsed by PBICompass, so they are excluded from this audit.

    8. Recommendations

    High Some relationships use bidirectional cross-filtering.PBIC-MOD-001

    Why it matters: Bidirectional filters can create ambiguous filter paths and materially slow down query performance.

    Suggested fix: Switch to single-direction filtering and use CROSSFILTER() in specific DAX measures where bidirectional behavior is truly needed.

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to resolve bidirectional cross-filtering
Model.Relationships.First(rel => rel.FromTable.Name == "Sales" && rel.ToTable.Name == "Date").CrossFilteringBehavior = CrossFilteringBehavior.OneDirection;

    Expected benefit: More predictable filtering and better query performance.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    High A bidirectional relationship filters a fact table.PBIC-MOD-015

    Why it matters: Bidirectional filtering on a fact table is the most common cause of ambiguous or duplicated totals, and carries a real performance cost as the model grows.

    Suggested fix: Switch the relationship to single-direction filtering from the dimension side, and use CROSSFILTER() in the specific measure(s) that genuinely need the reverse direction.

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to resolve bidirectional cross-filtering
Model.Relationships.First(rel => rel.FromTable.Name == "Sales" && rel.ToTable.Name == "Date").CrossFilteringBehavior = CrossFilteringBehavior.OneDirection;

    Expected benefit: Predictable, fast aggregation on the fact table.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    Medium Many measures/columns have no description.PBIC-DOC-002

    Why it matters: Missing descriptions mean new team members must reverse-engineer intent from DAX and column names alone.

    Suggested fix: Add descriptions to the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and self-documenting metadata in Power BI Desktop tooltips.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium Row-level security gaps were found.PBIC-GOV-001

    Why it matters: Without RLS (or with roles that have no members configured), sensitive rows may be visible to more users than intended.

    Suggested fix: Define RLS roles for any data that should be restricted, and assign members either in the model or in the Power BI Service.

    Expected benefit: Data access that matches the intended audience.

    Estimated effort: Medium

    Medium Description coverage across measures and columns is low.PBIC-GOV-002

    Why it matters: Missing descriptions increase onboarding time and the risk of misinterpreting a field's meaning.

    Suggested fix: Prioritize descriptions for the most-used measures and columns first.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misunderstandings.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Medium No owner or data classification was specified for this report.PBIC-GOV-003

    Why it matters: Without a named owner, there's no clear point of contact for questions, incidents, or change requests.

    Suggested fix: Assign a business owner and a data classification (e.g. Internal, Confidential) in the documentation metadata.

    Expected benefit: Clear accountability and appropriate handling of the data.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some measures have no description.PBIC-DAX-003

    Why it matters: Without a description, business meaning has to be reverse-engineered from the DAX every time.

    Suggested fix: Add a one-sentence description to each measure explaining what it calculates and why.

    Expected benefit: Faster onboarding and fewer misinterpretations of what a measure means.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some ID/key-style columns are visible to report authors.PBIC-MOD-006

    Why it matters: Visible technical join keys clutter the field list and can be mis-used in visuals by mistake.

    Suggested fix: Hide columns that exist only to support relationships.

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to hide key/ID columns
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["CustomerKey"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["OrderDateKey"].IsHidden = true;
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["ShipDateKey"].IsHidden = true;

    Expected benefit: A cleaner field list focused on business-relevant fields.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some calculated columns are never used in any report visual.PBIC-MOD-010

    Why it matters: Unused calculated columns still consume model size and refresh time for no analytical benefit.

    Suggested fix: Remove calculated columns that are not referenced anywhere, or confirm they are needed for row-level security/relationships.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and faster refreshes.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some relationships are inactive.PBIC-MOD-009

    Why it matters: Inactive relationships only take effect via USERELATIONSHIP() in DAX — easy to forget and easy to misconfigure.

    Suggested fix: Document where each inactive relationship is activated, or make it the active relationship if that's the common case.

    Expected benefit: Less risk of a report silently using the wrong relationship.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low Some visible columns look like high-cardinality identifiers.PBIC-PERF-002

    Why it matters: High-cardinality columns compress poorly and can slow down the model if used in visuals or relationships.

    Suggested fix: Hide the column if it's a technical key, or confirm its cardinality is acceptable for the intended use.

    Expected benefit: Smaller model size and better query performance, if confirmed unnecessary.

    Estimated effort: Low

    Low 12 unused asset(s) were found (measures, columns, tables, calculated columns, or hidden non-drillthrough pages).

    Why it matters: Unused assets add to model size, refresh time, and cognitive load for anyone maintaining the report.

    Suggested fix: Remove unused assets, or confirm they're intentionally kept for a documented reason (e.g. future use, RLS support).

    // Tabular Editor C# Script to delete unused assets
Model.Tables["Customer"].Columns["Customer Name"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Date"].Columns["Month Name"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["CustomerKey"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["OrderDateKey"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["ShipDateKey"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["Quantity"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["UnitPrice"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["Status"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Columns["LineTotal"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Measures["Revenue YTD"].Delete();
Model.Tables["Sales"].Measures["Orphan Margin"].Delete();

    Expected benefit: A leaner, easier-to-maintain model.

    Estimated effort: Low
